question: What is the definition of Longitude?

options.text: Lines of longitude are called meridians and divide the earth from pole to pole, with the 0° meridian passing through Greenwich, England.
Lines of longitude is the imaginary angular distance in degrees of a place from the equator, measured at the centre of the Earth and designated either North or South.
Longitude is also known as the equator.

classification_notes: Latitude and longitude are basic navigation concepts.
